title: Ongoing Drug Enforcement Efforts Lead to Four Arrests
content:
May 13, 2026
Type(s)
Police News
A recent investigation by the Fredericton Police Force’s Integrated Enforcement Unit (IEU) has resulted in the arrest of four Fredericton-area people and the seizure of significant quantities of illegal drugs, including cocaine, meth, and fentanyl.
On April 30, 2026, members of the IEU with support from FPF patrols, and K9 unit, arrested Bradley Webb, age 38 and Erica Trecartin, age 31, and seized the following items:
•    Approx. 313 grams of crystal methamphetamine
•    Approx. 27 grams of cocaine
•    Approx. 5 grams of fentanyl
•    Approx.  $2,700 in Canadian currency, believed to be proceeds of crime
•    Digital scales
Webb has been charged with possession of proceeds of crime, multiple counts of possession for the purpose of trafficking fentanyl, methamphetamine, and cocaine, as well as breaching a conditional sentence order.  Trecartin has been charged with operation while prohibited, breach of probation, and possession for the purpose of trafficking methamphetamine and cocaine.
Webb and Trecartin remain in custody and are currently remanded pending future court dates. At the time of their arrest, they were both facing prior drug trafficking-related charges and were out on conditions.
The two additional males, ages 36 and 25, were also arrested on scene and face trafficking related and possession related charges, respectively. Both met conditions for release pending future court dates and cannot be named as they have yet to appear in court.
This investigation reflects the ongoing efforts of the Fredericton Police Force and the New Brunswick Integrated Enforcement Unit to disrupt drug trafficking and reduce the presence of illegal drugs within the community.
– 30 –

title: New council to be sworn in on June 1
content:
May 12, 2026
Type(s)
City Hall
Fredericton residents have elected a new mayor and council following the May 11 municipal election.
The incoming city council includes Steve Hicks, former city councillor as mayor-elect, eight new councillors and four returning councillors, who will serve until May 2030.
This election marks the first time an equal number of men and women have been elected to serve as ward councillors.
The current mayor and council will remain in office until the swearing-in ceremony at City Hall on June 1. The final regular meeting of the current council will be held on May 19, 2026.
“Thank you to everyone who put their name forward to serve the City of Fredericton and to those who participated in the municipal election through voting” said Jennifer Lawson, City Clerk. “The city looks forward to welcoming the newly elected mayor and council and supporting them as they begin their term in the coming weeks.”
Following the swearing in ceremony on June 1, the deputy mayor and committee appointments will be announced on June 8.
The incoming council includes:
Mayor: Steve Hicks
Ward 1: Kate Hunter
Ward 2: Ryan Gregg
Ward 3: Colin Ebbett
Ward 4: Jocelyn Pike (incumbent)
Ward 5: Angela Hicks
Ward 6: P. Blair Sullivan
Ward 7: Josh Hambrook
Ward 8: Greg Ericson (incumbent, acclaimed)
Ward 9: Ruth Breen (incumbent)
Ward 10: Kelly Murray
Ward 11: Jason LeJeune (incumbent)
Ward 12: Janet E. Moser
Additional information including results and voter turnout is available on Elections NB’s
website
.

title: City hours of operation for Victoria Day 2026
content:
May 12, 2026
Type(s)
City Hall
The City of Fredericton is reminding residents of the following hours of operation for Victoria Day on Monday, May 18 2026.
City Hall and other administrative offices will be closed.
Regular garbage and recycling collection will occur. Routine schedules may vary, and residents are asked to place containers curbside by 7 a.m.
There will be no transit service on Monday, May 18, 2026.
The Beek Court Municipal Soil Management Facility is open on Monday, May 18. Curbside yard waste collection set for the week of May 25–29, 2026.
The Visitor Information Centre will be open from 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m.
The Fredericton Senior Centre will be open on Monday, May 18, from 9:30 a.m. to noon. Program schedules may vary. Residents are encouraged to visit here for more details.
The Nashwaaksis Field House will be closed on Sunday, May 17 and open on Monday, May 18, 2026, from 3:00 p.m. to 9:00 p.m.
City arenas and the Fredericton Indoor Pool will remain open during their usual operating hours. Extended public swim at the Fredericton Indoor Pool will take place on Monday, May 18, from 2:15 p.m. to 4:15 p.m.
Public skate times at indoor rinks are as follows:
Lady Beaverbrook Rink: 12:00 p.m. to 1:00 p.m.
To learn more about what is happening in the Fredericton Capital Region, visit
www.tourismfredericton.ca
and check out their calendar of events or follow Fredericton Tourism social media channels.
